provenance.source_corpus: Threshold dynamics in plant succession after tree planting in agricultural riparian zones

source_spans.text: Tree planting efficiently restored herbaceous forest communities in riparian zones by inducing a species turnover mediated by light availability corresponding to the threshold dynamics model in plant succession.
Once canopy cover passed a threshold of ca 40%, plant succession started and led to the re-establishment of forest communities 17 years after planting.

Tree planting efficiently restored herbaceous forest communities in riparian zones by inducing a species turnover mediated by light availability corresponding to the threshold dynamics model in plant succession.
As revealed by Principal Response Curves and broken stick models, herbaceous species composition was stable during the first 12 –13 years after tree planting, but then abruptly shifted.

Following herbaceous species turnover, the cover of ecological groups changed significantly towards covers of natural riparian forests: shade-tolerant species generally increased, while light-demanding and non-native species decreased.

Following herbaceous species turnover, the cover of ecological groups changed significantly towards covers of natural riparian forests: shade-tolerant species generally increased, while light-demanding and non-native species decreased.

Following herbaceous species turnover, the cover of ecological groups changed significantly towards covers of natural riparian forests: shade-tolerant species generally increased, while light-demanding and non-native species decreased.

During this turnover, shade-tolerant species associated with natural riparian forests increased at the expense of light-demanding species, while non-native species decreased but were replaced by native species characteristic of riparian natural forests only in field edge community

Tree planting efficiently restored herbaceous forest communities in riparian zones by inducing a species turnover mediated by light availability corresponding to the threshold dynamics model in plant succession.
